label: Cooked leftovers (meals, cooked meat, pasta, rice)
steps: Scrape leftovers from plates into the green bin or approved compostable liner. Remove all packaging, napkins and cutlery first.
notes: For very smelly meat or seafood scraps, freeze in a container and add to the green bin on collection day to reduce odour and pests.

label: Raw fruit & veg scraps (peels, cores, stalks)
stream: FOGO / green bin or home compost (if available)
steps: Place peels, cores, coffee grounds and eggshells directly into the green bin or home compost.
notes: Remove produce stickers, rubber bands and any plastic netting before disposal to avoid contamination.

label: Packaged food (opened or unopened)
stream: Split between organics and general waste / recycling, depending on packaging
steps: If safe, separate food from packaging: food goes to the green bin (if accepted for that food type); packaging goes to recycling or landfill according to the packaging label.
notes: If food is heavily spoiled, mouldy or unsafe to open, keep it in the packaging and place the whole item into the general‑waste bin.

label: Expired or spoiled food
stream: Depends on type: usually FOGO / green bin for solids, general waste for restricted liquids/fats
steps: If food is just past its date but still safe, prioritise using it in recipes. If spoiled, follow cooked/raw rules for solids. Avoid pouring large amounts of liquid fats or oils down the sink; place cooled fats in a container and bin as general waste.
notes: Always check local council FOGO rules for what liquids, fats and dairy they accept. When in doubt, use general waste for liquids and high‑fat residues.
